name: libsub-infix-perl
title: libsub-infix-perl
description:
create a fake infix operator
Sub::Infix creates fake infix operators using overloading. It doesn't
use source filters, or Devel::Declare, or any of that magic. (Though
Devel::Declare isn't magic enough to define infix operators anyway; I
know; I've tried.) It's pure Perl, has no non-core dependencies, and
runs on Perl 5.8.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libsub-infix-perl because create a fake infix operator.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libsub-infix-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libsub-infix-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libsub-infix-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
